prompt: isometric pixel-art game tiles for a top-down RPG map. Each tile is a DIFFERENT large object standing FREE on a small flat patch of dark near-black volcanic rock — the object itself rises straight up and IS the sprite. Do NOT put it on a raised block, cube, column, box, crate or pedestal, and do NOT draw the ground as a tall raised cube: just a small low patch at the base, everything else transparent. Go for maximum variety — different shapes, species, ages and conditions, no two tiles alike. The set: 1) a cluster of black stone menhirs 2) a tall obsidian tower 3) a tall lava-rock chimney 4) a tall bundle of basalt organ pipes 5) a tall scorched snag 6) a black rock arch 7) a dark volcanic spire with glowing cracks 8) a jagged obsidian monolith 9) a cluster of black basalt columns 10) a tall smoking fumarole cone. cozy hand-crafted top-down RPG pixel art, rich natural texture, cohesive harmonious palette, soft directional shading, high quality game asset, transparent background
